overview: Converts the character set encoding of an input document and optionally updates encoding declarations in XML and HTML files. The step can un-escape various character reference notations (NCR, CER, Java-style) on input, and re-escape characters using a choice of notations on output. It operates on raw documents and produces raw documents.

unescapeNCR.help:
Un-escapes all types of **numeric character references** (NCRs) when reading the input document.

For example, `ÿ`, `á`, and `&#e1;` are all un-escaped to the literal character `Ăˇ`.

If disabled, any NCR in the input document remains in the exact same form in the output.

unescapeCER.help:
Un-escapes standard **HTML character entity references** (CERs) when reading the input document.

For example, `á` is un-escaped to `Ăˇ`.

If disabled, any CER in the input document remains in the exact same form in the output.
notes: The five XML-reserved entity references `&`, `<`, `>`, `'`, and `"` are **never** un-escaped, as they may need to be preserved regardless of encoding in HTML and XML documents.

unescapeJava.help:
Un-escapes **Java-style** escaped characters (`\uXXXX`) when reading the input document.

For example, `\u00e1` and `\u00E1` are both un-escaped to `Ăˇ`.

If disabled, all Java-style escaped characters remain in the exact same form in the output.

escapeAll.help:
Controls which characters are escaped in the output:

- **Disabled** (default): Only characters not supported by the output encoding are escaped.
- **Enabled**: All extended characters (non-ASCII) are escaped, regardless of whether the output encoding supports them.

escapeNotation.help: Selects the escape notation used when characters must be escaped in the output.
escapeNotation.values:
Integer selecting the notation:
- `0` â€” Uppercase hexadecimal NCR (e.g. `á`)
- `1` â€” Lowercase hexadecimal NCR (e.g. `á`)
- `2` â€” Decimal NCR (e.g. `ÿ`)
- `3` â€” Character entity reference (e.g. `á`). Falls back to uppercase hex NCR if no entity is defined for the character.
- `4` â€” Uppercase Java-style (e.g. `\u00E1`)
- `5` â€” Lowercase Java-style (e.g. `\u00e1`)
- `6` â€” User-defined notation (uses `userFormat`)

userFormat.help:
A **Java format expression** that takes an integer value and produces the escaped output. Only used when `escapeNotation` is set to `6` (user-defined).

The integer value is the **Unicode code point** of the character to escape (unless `useBytes` is enabled).
userFormat.values: Any valid Java `String.format()` expression accepting one integer argument. Use `%d` for decimal, `%x` for lowercase hex, `%X` for uppercase hex.

dependsOn.condition: must be 6 (user-defined) for this to take effect

examples: `\x%02X` â€” custom hex notation with zero-padding

useBytes.help:
When enabled, the values passed to the `userFormat` expression are the **byte values of the output encoding** rather than Unicode code points.

This is useful when the target format expects encoding-specific byte escapes rather than Unicode escapes.

dependsOn.condition: must be 6 (user-defined) for this to take effect
defines the format expression that receives the byte values

examples: With format `\'%x` and Shift-JIS output: `ć—Ąćś¬čŞž` â†’ `\'93\'fa\'96\'7b\'8c\'ea` (byte values)
Without this option: `ć—Ąćś¬čŞž` â†’ `\'65e5\'672c\'8a9e` (Unicode code points)

BOMonUTF8.help:
Adds a **Byte-Order-Mark** (BOM) at the beginning of UTF-8 output files.

The BOM (`U+FEFF`) is sometimes required by certain tools or specifications but is generally optional for UTF-8. Some systems may misinterpret a BOM as visible characters.
notes: Only applies when the output encoding is UTF-8.

reportUnsupported.help:
Logs all characters that are not supported by the output encoding. Useful for identifying potential data loss when converting to a more limited encoding.

Unsupported characters are listed in the pipeline log output.

processingNotes: Encoding declaration updates are performed on the **first 1024 characters** of each document only.
The declaration update routine is **not** XML/HTML-aware â€” it cannot distinguish between normal code and comments. Encoding declarations inside comments may be incorrectly updated.
A document can be both an XML and HTML document and have **both** types of encoding/charset declarations updated.
For XML documents: if an XML declaration exists, its encoding attribute is updated; if only a `.xml` extension is present, a full XML declaration is prepended.
For HTML documents: if a charset declaration exists, it is updated; if a `.htm*` extension is present, a `<meta charset>` is inserted after `<head>` or synthesized after `<html>`.
limitations: Declaration detection uses simple string matching on the first 1024 chars â€” not a real XML/HTML parser.
Cannot distinguish encoding declarations inside comments from real declarations.
HTML charset insertion heuristic relies on finding `<head>` or `<html` strings, which may fail with unusual document structures.

examples.title: Convert Latin-1 to UTF-8 with NCR un-escaping
examples.description: Converts a Latin-1 encoded file to UTF-8, un-escaping all numeric character references and character entity references in the process. The BOM is added to the UTF-8 output.
examples.config:
```yaml
unescapeNCR: true
unescapeCER: true
unescapeJava: false
escapeAll: false
escapeNotation: 0
BOMonUTF8: true
reportUnsupported: true
```

examples.title: Custom byte-level escaping for Shift-JIS
examples.description: Uses a user-defined format with byte values to produce encoding-specific escape sequences for a Shift-JIS output file.
examples.config:
```yaml
unescapeNCR: true
unescapeCER: true
unescapeJava: true
escapeAll: true
escapeNotation: 6
userFormat: "\\x%02X"
useBytes: true
BOMonUTF8: false
reportUnsupported: true
```
